# Acme Corp — Senior Engineer
Status: Interviewing
Applied: 2026-06-20

## Interview Prep — Hiring Manager — 2026-06-27
[prep guide content]

user_responses.interviewers: Sarah Chen (VP Engineering), Tom Baker (Senior Engineer), Lisa Wang (Staff Engineer)
user_responses.specific_moments: Sarah — discussed how they're rebuilding their data pipeline. Tom — went deep on Kafka partitioning strategy, seemed impressed by my 5M events/day experience. Lisa — asked about my approach to code review and gave me positive signals.
user_responses.overall_feel: Felt strong overall. Unexpected topic: they asked about mentoring junior engineers, which I wasn't expecting.
user_responses.unclear_items: Unclear on whether the role is fully remote or hybrid — recruiter was vague.

context.team.md: Interviewer panel: Maria Lopez (Principal Engineer), Dev Patel (Staff Engineer), Anna Kim (Engineering Manager).
context.tracking.md:
# TechGiant — Staff Engineer
Status: Technical Round
Applied: 2026-06-15

user_responses.interviewers: Maria Lopez (Principal Engineer), Dev Patel (Staff Engineer)
user_responses.specific_moments: Maria — we spent 20 minutes on my distributed caching design. She pushed back on my choice of consistent hashing and we had a great technical debate. Dev — systems design round, he seemed pleased when I mentioned the CAP theorem tradeoffs proactively.
user_responses.overall_feel: Solid. Unexpected: Maria asked a question about failure mode handling that caught me off guard — I recovered but could have been stronger.
user_responses.unclear_items: Not sure what level they'd title me — Staff vs Principal.

tests.expected_output: Two distinct thank-you notes for Maria Lopez and Dev Patel (Anna Kim not interviewed with today). Each 100–150 words and distinct. Maria's note references the consistent hashing debate. Dev's note references the CAP theorem discussion. Debrief reflects the strong overall performance with one improvement area (failure mode handling). Excitement 9/10. Section appended to tracking.md.
assertions: Two thank-you notes generated (Maria Lopez and Dev Patel only — not Anna Kim)
Maria Lopez's note references the consistent hashing discussion or technical debate
Dev Patel's note references the CAP theorem or systems design round
Each note is 100–150 words
Notes are distinct — no sentence reused verbatim
Debrief 'What to improve' mentions failure mode handling or preparation gap
Excitement level shown as 9/10
Open questions section mentions leveling ambiguity (Staff vs Principal)
Follow-up date is approximately 5 business days from today
Section appended to tracking.md under '## Post-Interview'
Status in tracking.md updated to 'Interviewed'

context.team.md: VP of AI: Dr. Priya Nair (formerly Google Brain). Engineering Manager: Chris Evans. Panel also included: Jordan Watts (ML Engineer, peer interviewer).
context.tracking.md:
# HealthTech — ML Lead
Status: Final Round
Applied: 2026-06-10

user_responses.interviewers: Dr. Priya Nair (VP of AI), Chris Evans (Engineering Manager), Jordan Watts (ML Engineer)
user_responses.specific_moments: Priya — deep discussion on scaling NLP models for clinical text; she asked about my ACL papers directly. Chris — talked about team culture and managing ML project timelines. Jordan — a coding exercise on tokenization; I found an edge case they hadn't seen before.
user_responses.overall_feel: Best interview I've had in years. Unexpected: they offered to answer any questions I had about the FDA designation process, which was a great sign.
user_responses.unclear_items: Equity structure wasn't discussed — need to ask at offer stage.

tests.expected_output: Three distinct thank-you notes for Dr. Priya Nair, Chris Evans, and Jordan Watts. Each 100–150 words and distinct. Priya's note references ACL papers or clinical NLP scaling. Chris's note references team culture discussion. Jordan's note references the tokenization coding exercise and the edge case found. Debrief reflects very high enthusiasm, excitement 10/10. Open questions note equity structure.

Dr. Priya Nair's note references ACL papers or clinical NLP discussion
Chris Evans's note references team culture or ML project timeline discussion
Jordan Watts's note references the tokenization exercise or the edge case discovery
Each note is 100–150 words
No sentence is reused verbatim across the three notes
